ul.li: Offset 12 falls outside the direct block range (0-11).
The indirect pointer is checked and found to be NULL.
Result: Allocation required to resolve the hole.
block_bitmap_alloc(fs) searches for the first free bit.
Physical Block 5000 is selected and marked as used.
This block will store the actual file data.
Logical offset 12 requires a translation table (Single Indirect).
Since inode->indirect == 0, we allocate Block 4999 for this purpose.
Block is initialized with zeros to prevent garbage pointers.
The physical address of the data block (5000) is stored in the first slot (ptr_0) of the indirect block (4999).
inode->blocks updated to 16 to count both data and indirect metadata.
Buffers marked dirty in the cache.
fsync() triggers write-back of metadata (4999 and Inode) and data (5000).
The block is now accessible via standard lookup.
